event: presented with severe and constant bilateral lower extremity pain
headache associated with occasional episodes of diplopia
chronic alcohol intake (four 8 oz cans of beer daily for twenty years)
physical examination demonstrated somnolent but arousable
decreased muscle strength of bilateral lower extremities
CT brain: multiple intraaxial hyperdense lesions with vasogenic edema
CT chest: bilateral cavitary nodules and right hilar lymphadenopathy
MRI brain: multiple rim enhancing lesions with vasogenic edema
BAL obtained from left upper lobe
left upper extremity weakness by day 12
right upper extremity weakness by day 13
right lower facial weakness by day 13
repeat head-CT with contrast: multiple ring-enhancing lesions
microscopic foci of necrosis with acute inflammation
brain macroscopic findings: multiple bilateral cavities
GMS stain positive for gram-positive branching rods
final diagnosis: nocardia pneumonitis and encephalitis
